_meta.description: Richer text used by `agents/topic_classifier.py` when computing the centroid embedding for each topic. The bare topic name (e.g. 'division') is a single word — its embedding doesn't strongly resemble a real student message ('Peter bought chocolate, has 350g of crumbs, each bar is 80g, max bars?'). Embedding the description below instead gives the centroid more semantic surface area to match against.
_meta.fallback: Topics not listed here fall back to embedding the bare canonical name. Add new entries when you observe live messages that should classify under a topic but don't.
_meta.format: Each value should be ONE LINE: 'topic — informal definition + 3-5 keywords / synonyms / common-context phrases that real student messages tend to use.'

addition: addition — combining numbers to find a total. Adding amounts together, finding sums. Word problems about putting things together, totals, altogether, in all.
subtraction: subtraction — taking away from a number, finding the difference. How much is left, how many more, how much further. Word problems about losing, removing, having less than, comparing two amounts.
multiplication: multiplication — finding a total when you have equal groups. Times tables, repeated addition, area of rectangles. Word problems about 'X groups of Y', 'each contains', 'per'.
division: division — splitting things into equal groups, sharing equally between people, finding how many groups of one number fit into another. Word problems about distributing, dividing, sharing, 'how many per', 'split between'.
fractions: fractions — parts of a whole, numerator and denominator, halves, thirds, quarters. Slices of pizza, sharing into equal parts, comparing parts, mixed numbers, improper fractions.
decimals: decimals — numbers with a decimal point, tenths, hundredths, money in dollars and cents, rounding decimal values, comparing decimal amounts.
place value: place value — what each digit means in a number based on its position, ones tens hundreds thousands, expanded form, regrouping, base-10 representation.
shapes: shapes — recognizing and naming geometric figures, squares circles triangles rectangles, sides and corners, sorting shapes by properties.
measurement: measurement — measuring lengths weights and volumes, units like inches centimeters grams liters, comparing sizes, converting between units, reading rulers and scales.
time and money: time and money — telling time on a clock, hours and minutes, dollars and cents, counting coins and bills, making change, calculating elapsed time.
patterns: patterns — identifying repeating sequences, what comes next in a pattern, color or shape patterns, number patterns, skip counting.
counting: counting — counting objects one by one, counting forward or backward, counting by twos / fives / tens, comparing quantities, more or fewer.
ratios and proportions: ratios and proportions — comparing two quantities, ratio notation like 3:5, equivalent ratios, scale, finding a missing value in a proportion.
percentages: percentages — parts per hundred, calculating a percent of a number, percent increase or decrease, discounts and tax, expressing fractions as percents.
negative numbers: negative numbers — numbers less than zero, integer arithmetic, debts and below-zero temperatures, the number line, adding and subtracting negatives.
exponents: exponents — powers of a number, squared and cubed, exponent rules, scientific notation, repeated multiplication, base and exponent.
expressions: expressions — algebraic expressions with variables, evaluating expressions, combining like terms, simplifying, distributing, no equals sign needed.
linear equations: linear equations — solving for an unknown variable in an equation like 3x + 5 = 14, isolating the variable, equations with one variable, balancing both sides.
linear functions: linear functions — straight-line relationships, slope and y-intercept, the equation y = mx + b, graphing lines, finding slope from two points.
systems of equations: systems of equations — two equations with two unknowns, solving by substitution or elimination, finding where two lines intersect.
quadratic equations: quadratic equations — equations with x squared, factoring, completing the square, the quadratic formula, finding roots, discriminant.
quadratic functions: quadratic functions — parabolas, the U-shape graph, vertex and axis of symmetry, opens up or down, the function f(x) = ax² + bx + c.
polynomials: polynomials — expressions with multiple terms and powers, polynomial degree, adding subtracting and multiplying polynomials, FOIL.
factoring: factoring — breaking an expression into a product of factors, common factor, factoring trinomials, difference of squares, GCF.
radicals: radicals — square roots and higher roots, simplifying radicals, rationalizing the denominator, operations with radical expressions.
rational expressions: rational expressions — fractions where the numerator and denominator are polynomials, simplifying rational expressions, multiplying and dividing them, finding common denominators with variables.
inequalities: inequalities — comparing values with less-than, greater-than, less-than-or-equal, greater-than-or-equal, solving and graphing inequalities, flipping the sign when multiplying by a negative.
absolute value: absolute value — distance from zero, the absolute value bars, solving equations and inequalities with absolute values.
functions: functions — input-output relationships, function notation f(x), domain and range, evaluating functions, function composition, identifying functions from graphs.
geometry triangles: geometry triangles — three-sided shapes, types of triangles, angle sums, the Pythagorean theorem, similar and congruent triangles.
geometry circles: geometry circles — circumference and area of a circle, radius and diameter, pi, central and inscribed angles, arcs and chords.
geometry transformations: geometry transformations — translations rotations reflections and dilations, congruence and similarity, mapping a figure onto another.
geometry area and perimeter: geometry area and perimeter — finding the distance around a shape and the space inside, area of rectangles triangles circles, perimeter of polygons.
volume: volume — measuring the space inside a 3D figure, volume of cubes prisms cylinders cones spheres, units like cubic centimeters.
trigonometry basics: trigonometry basics — sine cosine tangent in right triangles, SOHCAHTOA, finding missing sides and angles, special right triangles.
trigonometry: trigonometry — the unit circle, sine cosine tangent for any angle, radians and degrees, trig identities, solving triangles, law of sines and cosines.
trigonometric identities: trigonometric identities — Pythagorean identity, double-angle formulas, sum and difference formulas, proving identities, simplifying trig expressions.
exponential functions: exponential functions — functions of the form f(x) = a · b^x, exponential growth and decay, compound interest, doubling time.
logarithms: logarithms — the inverse of exponentials, log base b, log rules, solving equations with logs, natural logarithm ln.
sequences and series: sequences and series — ordered lists of numbers, arithmetic and geometric sequences, common difference and common ratio, summation, sigma notation.
complex numbers: complex numbers — numbers with a real and imaginary part, the imaginary unit i where i² = -1, adding and multiplying complex numbers.
vectors: vectors — quantities with both magnitude and direction, vector addition and scalar multiplication, dot product, components.
matrices: matrices — rectangular arrays of numbers, matrix addition and multiplication, determinant, inverse matrix, solving systems with matrices.
limits: limits — the value a function approaches as the input approaches some number, evaluating limits, one-sided limits, limits at infinity, the foundation of calculus.
derivatives: derivatives — the rate of change of a function, slope of the tangent line, differentiation rules, power rule chain rule product rule.
integrals: integrals — the area under a curve, antiderivatives, definite and indefinite integrals, integration techniques, fundamental theorem of calculus.
differential equations: differential equations — equations involving derivatives, finding a function given a rate of change, separation of variables, initial value problems.
linear algebra: linear algebra — vector spaces and linear transformations, matrices and determinants, eigenvalues and eigenvectors, systems of linear equations.
multivariable calculus: multivariable calculus — calculus with functions of several variables, partial derivatives, multiple integrals, gradients, vector calculus.
statistics: statistics — collecting analyzing and interpreting data, mean median mode, standard deviation, distributions, sample size, hypothesis testing.
probability: probability — the chance of an event happening, sample space, independent and conditional probability, dice and cards, probability distributions.
probability basics: probability basics — what's the chance, fractions of likelihood, picking marbles or rolling dice, simple events.
combinatorics: combinatorics — counting arrangements and combinations, permutations and combinations, factorials, binomial coefficients, the multiplication principle.
data and graphs: data and graphs — bar graphs, line plots, pictographs, reading data from charts, organizing information, interpreting tables.
data and statistics: data and statistics — bar graphs and pie charts, mean median mode, range, comparing data sets, drawing conclusions from data.
